title: Sarafur

'''Sarafur''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]], outside of which it was also known as '''Kuna's Tail III''' due to its location in the [[Kuna's Tail system/Legends|Kuna's Tail system]]. The planet was tidally locked with its [[Star/Legends|sun]], with the same parts of its [[Desert/Legends|desert]]-covered surface always facing the star as a result. Sarafur's surface was dotted with ancient [[Stone/Legends|stone]] ruins, and in the zone separating the illuminated and dark sides of the planet lay the dried-out [[Water/Legends|seabed]] known as the [[Krasho Salt Rifts]], which was the site of monolithic [[Salt/Legends|salt]] formations, calcified [[Coral/Legends|corals]], and [[Skeleton/Legends|skeletons]] of large aquatic creatures.

At one point, Sarafur was inhabited by a [[Unidentified Sarafur species|sentient species]] that lived in a bustling [[City/Legends|city]] near the planet's twilight region. The [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]] of the planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] also once [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] the world, establishing hundreds of nests there, but the former [[Species/Legends|species]] eventually went extinct, and the Killiks abandoned Sarafur at some point by [[30,000 BBY]]. When a [[Unidentified Killik Sith warrior|Killik Sith warrior]] was driven off the world [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]], he found refuge on Sarafur in a [[Killik Sith warrior's tomb|building]] that eventually became his [[Burial/Legends|tomb]]. During the [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] leading up to [[25,200 BBY]], the planet was a part of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]].

By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the ruins on Sarafur were home to grave-[[Thief/Legends|robbers]], [[Prospector/Legends|prospectors]], [[Treasure hunting/Legends|treasure hunters]], and fugitives as well as several groups of criminals. Eventually, rumors claimed that Sarafur was the resting place of either or both of the [[Alsakan Tessent|two]] [[Ayrou Tessent|artifacts]] known as the [[Tessent]]s, and at some point by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], a group of treasure [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]] investigated the Killik [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] warrior's tomb during their search for the artifacts.

==Description==
Kuna's Tail III was a small terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the third [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position around the [[Kuna's Tail system/Legends|Kuna's Tail system's]] [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Star/Legends|star]].<ref name="Endor" /> It lay within the [[Outer Moddell systems]] area of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]],<ref name="EAOC KunaTail" /> one of the [[Sector/Legends|subsectors]] making up the [[Inner Zuma Region]]<ref name="TEA" /> within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Zuma sector/Legends|Zuma sector]].<ref name="EAOC KunaTail" /> The planet was connected via [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] to the [[Kuna's Fist system/Legends|Kuna's Fist system]].<ref name="TEA" /> The Kuna's Tail [[Star system/Legends|system]] was so named since it formed a part of the [[Kuna constellation]], as seen and named by the [[Ayrou/Legends|Ayrou]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of the planet [[Maya Kovel/Legends|Maya Kovel]].<ref name="Endor" /> In the Moddell sector, Kuna's Tail III was known as Sarafur.<ref name="TEA" />

Sarafur had standard [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]], a breathable Type I [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and a [[Temperature/Legends|temperate]]<ref name="Endor" />-to-hot climate.<ref name="Killiks of Sarafur" /> The planet was tidally locked in its orbit around its sun, with the same parts of its surface always facing the star.<ref name="Endor" /> The border between the light and dark regions of Sarafur featured dusty and forbidding landscapes<ref name="Tessent" /> and was the only inhabitable part of the planet. Sarafur's surface was covered in<ref name="Endor" /> desolate and dangerous<ref name="Tessent" /> [[Desert/Legends|desert]].<ref name="Endor" /> Certain [[Astronomer/Legends|astronomers]] theorized that Sarafur had become tidally locked so rapidly that the planet's [[Water/Legends|sea]]—which eventually gave way to the [[Krasho Salt Rifts]]—had evaporated in as quickly as several [[Local year|years]] or even just [[Standard month/Legends|months]].<ref name="Tessent" />

==History==
===The Killiks of Sarafur===
[[File:Sarafur-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Sarafur and its surroundings during the reign of the Infinite Empire]]
At one point, Sarafur served as a [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] world for the [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]], a species of [[Hive mind/Legends|hive-minded]] [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoids]] that [[Homeworld/Legends|originated]] on the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. The Killiks were transplanted off their homeworld by the powerful [[Celestial/Legends|Celestials]], and ultimately both species disappeared from the known [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by [[30,000 BBY]]. Between approximately that date and [[25,200 BBY]], Sarafur fell under the control of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] of the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] species.<ref name="TEA" />

When a [[Unidentified Killik Sith warrior|Killik Sith warrior]] fled persecution on the Core Worlds planet [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] long before [[Human/Legends|Humans]] settled there<ref name="Tessent" /> between [[27,000 BBY/Legends|27,000 BBY]] and [[26,000 BBY/Legends|26,000 BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> he took up refuge on Sarafur. After his [[Death/Legends|death]], the [[Sith/Legends|Sith's]] [[Killik Sith warrior's tomb|tomb]] became a site strong in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], and his arrival on the desert world came to be the subject of an old [[Shistavanen/Legends|Shistavanen]] legend. At some point, the Alsakani [[Dark side adept/Legends|dark side adept]] [[Jilst Bindalin]] learned of the legend and located the [[Burial/Legends|tomb]]. After he stole a certain artifact, Bindalin hired a Shistavanen [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] to transport him and the artifact to Sarafur, and the Alsakani was never seen again after arriving there.<ref name="Tessent" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic}}
Around [[3643 BBY]],<ref name="3643 BBY">Per the reasoning [[Template:TOR_dates|here]], Act I of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place around [[3643 BBY]]. Since players of ''The Old Republic'' can assign their companions on the mission "Killiks of Sarafur" independently of the main class storyline at any point during the [[Star Wars video games|game]], the mission must take place at some point around 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref> an individual affiliated with one of the sides in the then-ongoing [[Sith Wars/Legends|struggle]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] sent a companion to Sarafur to investigate the long-abandoned Killik nests there. The companion discovered the belongings of long-lost [[Joiner]]s<ref name="Killiks of Sarafur" />—individuals who had been assimilated into the Killik hive mind<ref name="TEA" />—as well as a quantity of old, [[Crystal/Legends|crystallized]] [[Membrosia/Legends|membrosia]],<ref name="Killiks of Sarafur" /> a type of Killik-produced<ref name="TUR">''[[The Unknown Regions]]''</ref> [[Liquor/Legends|liquor]].<ref name="CSWE membrosia">{{CSWECite|II|305|membrosia}}</ref>
{{Endgame}}

===Hunting the Tessents===
{{RPG|"[[Race for the Tessent]]"}}
[[File:AlsakanTessent-Gamer9.png|thumb|right|295px|Sarafur came to figure in rumors and legends connected to the two artifacts known as the Tessents (the Alsakan Tessent pictured).]]
Eventually, rumors circulating in various [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]] began claiming that either—or both—of the [[Alsakan Tessent|two]] [[Ayrou Tessent|artifacts]] known as the [[Tessent]]s were located on Sarafur. In reality, they were on the Moddell sector worlds [[Ast Kikorie/Legends|Ast Kikorie]] and [[Vasha/Legends|Vasha]]. According to the rumors, at least one of the Tessents had come into the possession of a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime boss]] or a [[spice lord]] who resided somewhere in Sarafur's ruins. Occasionally, [[Treasure hunting/Legends|treasure hunters]] instead related stories about either of the artifacts supposedly falling into the hands of Sarafur's grave-[[Thief/Legends|robbers]] or coming to rest in a crypt of an [[Alien/Legends|alien]] ruler that was in turn located beneath the hideout of one of the planet's crime [[Lord/Legends|lords]]. Although [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Follnor Callat]], a [[Xenoarchaeology/Legends|xenoarchaeologist]] interested in obtaining the Tessent originating on Alsakan, was not aware of the legend concerning the arrival of the Killik Sith on Sarafur, he did know of another tale about a [[Force ghost/Legends|dark side spirit]] that resided in the planet's ruins.<ref name="Tessent" />

At some point by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="3 ABY">By default, the events of {{GamerCite|9|Race for the Tessent}} are set during the opposition to [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Slavery/Legends|enslavement]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars (1977) 69|''Star Wars'' (1977) 69]], which depict the [[Battle of the City of Bone|end]] of the Imperial presence on Mandalore per {{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}, to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> the rogue [[LC "hunting droid"|assassin droid]] [[LC-Zed]], who had fled from<ref name="Tessent" /> the neighboring<ref name="Endor" /> [[Spar sector]] after going on a killing spree there, sought temporary refuge from pursuing [[Abbaji System Constables|law enforcement forces]] on Sarafur, where it arrived aboard a stolen [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] and subsequently sought the means to repair the starship in order to travel offworld again. Around the same [[Time/Legends|time]], the [[Vashan Liberation Force]], a group of approximately twenty [[Vashan]]s intent on organizing a revolt against the Ayrou control over their homeworld Vasha, was formed on Sarafur. In addition, a group of treasure [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]] also arrived on the planet around that time while searching for the Tessents. The group encountered the [[Shipjacker/Legends|shipjacker]] [[Tershan]] and his crew and explored the Killik Sith's tomb, where it discovered a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]].<ref name="Tessent" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
[[File:Killik-TUR.jpg|thumb|left|290px|The Killiks (a Killik pictured) once colonized Sarafur.]]
At one point, Sarafur was inhabited by a [[Unidentified Sarafur species|sentient species]] that had gone extinct eons prior to the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Tessent" /> The planet was also inhabited by members of the Killik species once, although they ultimately abandoned the world.<ref name="Killiks of Sarafur" /> By the time of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]],<ref name="Endor" /> which began in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]],<ref name="TEA" /> Sarafur was home to 150,000 individuals of various species. Although Sarafur's population had access to [[Realspace/Legends|space]]-[[Technology level|level]] technology and the planet served as a [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]], it had no imports or exports of note. It had no overarching government<ref name="Endor" /> and was a lawless backwater world<ref name="Tessent" /> where death was a common occurrence.<ref name="Endor" />

Various bands of criminals made Sarafur their primary base of operations. Among the groups that resided the longest in Sarafur's ruins were the [[Outlaw tech/Legends|outlaw]] [[Armorer/Legends|armorers]] and [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] known as the [[Xal 3 Armorers' Confederacy]]. After being displaced from<ref name="Tessent" /> the adjacent<ref name="Endor" /> [[Ablajeck sector/Legends|Ablajeck sector]] by a feud with other outlaw techs, the group led by the [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Kallukoras]] established a presence on Sarafur. Another gang operating out of the desert planet was the [[Kintan Gunrunners]], a group of weapons [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] known throughout [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]], the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]], and other areas of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Primarily composed of [[Nikto/Legends|Niktos]] of various [[Subspecies/Legends|subspecies]], the gang employed—especially on Sarafur—members of other species well-adapted for the planet's desert heat, such as [[Kamarian]]s, [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'leks]], and [[Weequay/Legends|Weequays]]. The Gunrunners section based on Sarafur was led by the [[Kajain'sa'Nikto/Legends|Kajain'sa'Nikto]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] [[Tu'rsc'yulir]].<ref name="Tessent" />

==Locations==
===Ruins of Sarafur===
Sarafur's surface was dotted with unusual ancient ruins,<ref name="TEA" /> with many of those structures, hewn out of gigantic blocks of [[Stone/Legends|stone]], being located on the border between Sarafur's illuminated and dark sides.<ref name="Endor" /> The ruins were scoured by grave robbers,<ref name="Tessent" /> and [[Prospector/Legends|prospectors]], treasure hunters, and fugitives also used many of the structures as boltholes, with Sarafur accumulating over time a history of harboring various underworld figures.<ref name="Endor" />

The Xal 3 Armorers' Confederacy at one point established a base in the ruins, where the group was visited each [[Standard day/Legends|standard day]] by several starships of individuals interested in the Confederacy's [[Armor/Legends|armor]] design and repair services. The Vashan Liberation Force also maintained a small base that was hidden atop a series of collapsed multi-story ruins.<ref name="Tessent" /> In addition, hundreds of Killik nests had been abandoned on Sarafur [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] prior to<ref name="Killiks of Sarafur" /> 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].<ref name="3643 BBY" /> The nests were well-preserved by the planet's hot climate.<ref name="Killiks of Sarafur" /> By the time of the Yuuzhan Vong War, three [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]] of limited services were also located on Sarafur.<ref name="Endor" />

===Krasho Salt Rifts===
The former seabed known as the Krasho Salt Rifts lay on the meridian dividing the illuminated and dark sides of Sarafur. It extended for hundreds of [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] and covered the entire twilight region for several degrees of latitude. The area resembled a bright spot when viewed from the planet's orbit. The Krasho Salt Rifts were littered with monolithic crystalline [[Salt/Legends|salt]] formations, calcified [[Coral/Legends|corals]], and fossilized [[Skeleton/Legends|skeletons]] of colossal sea creatures. The Salt Rifts had dried out [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] before the reign of the Galactic Empire, with [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] sometimes speculating that a rapid drying out of the area had led to a complete extinction of its aquatic ecosystem before any of the species there had time to adapt.<ref name="Tessent" />

[[File:Kintan_Gunrunners.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Kintan Gunrunners gang operated out of the Krasho Salt Rifts.]]
The Kintan Gunrunners gang of smugglers maintained their main base in a series of ruins stretching along the Salt Rifts, a short distance into Sarafur's sun-lit portion. The Gunrunners had selected the location due to the privacy offered by the high temperatures in the area and kept their starships in an old arena situated deep within the ruins. The [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] shipjacker Tershan and his crew also operated out of the Salt Rifts, although they planned to establish a more secure base in a dense cluster of ruins situated several kilometers to the north of their temporary location. In addition, [[Arcona/Legends|Arcona]] criminal groups occasionally [[Mining/Legends|mined]] the Salt Rifts' crystal spires due to the species' pervasive addiction to salt.<ref name="Tessent" />

At one point, a bustling [[City/Legends|city]] of the species that had once inhabited Sarafur was situated at what eventually became the edge of the Krasho Salt Rifts, beyond the future base of the Xal 3 Armorers' Confederacy. The city included a building housing baths of [[Sulfur/Legends|sulfurous]] [[mineral water]]s. After a Killik Sith came to reside there and subsequently died on the planet, the building became his tomb. Tons of collapsing columns and walls eventually buried the baths, and they became one of the exceedingly few reservoirs of standing water in Sarafur's twilight zone that were located close to the planet's surface.<ref name="Tessent" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Publication history===
Sarafur first appeared in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure "[[Race for the Tessent]]," which was published for use with [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' in the [[Star Wars Gamer 9|ninth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Tessent" /> on [[February 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20020222.html|text=Starfighters Soar in Gamer #9}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed Sarafur in grid square H-16.<ref name="TEA" /> The planet was mentioned in a Scavenging Crew Skill mission of the [[2011]] [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', although the information regarding the mission's outcome was eventually removed from the game.<ref name="Killiks of Sarafur" />

[[File:Race_for_the_Tessent_G9.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Sarafur was introduced as a potential setting for the roleplaying adventure "Race for the Tessent."]]
Instead of featuring a set plot, "Race for the Tessent" presents several settings in the Moddell sector, Sarafur being one of them, that the player characters may visit during their search for the artifacts known as the Tessents. For each of the settings, the adventure also suggests possible circumstances for a Tessent being hidden there, with the section dedicated to Sarafur noting how, at some point, Jilst Bindalin could have stolen the Alsakan Tessent and brought it to the Killik Sith warrior's tomb on the planet. Assuming that as much of the non-contradictory information presented in the adventure as possible is [[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, this article assumes that the Alsakan Tessent and the Ayrou Tessent were instead hidden on the planets Ast Kikorie and Vasha, respectively.<ref name="Tessent" />

===Contradictions===
"Race for the Tessent" introduced an apparent contradiction regarding the dating of the Killik Sith warrior's arrival on Sarafur. The adventure establishes that the Sith traveled from the planet Alsakan to Sarafur long before Humans had colonized the former world, yet it also states that Humans drove the Killik off Alsakan. In line with the assumptions this article makes that are outlined above, it is also assumed that the latter statement is in error.<ref name="Tessent" />

[[File:Endor_and_the_Moddell_Sector_SWG9.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Along with "Race for the Tessent," the article "Endor and the Moddell Sector" introduced several contradictions relating to the description of Sarafur.]]
In addition, both the adventure<ref name="Tessent" /> and the roleplaying source article "[[Endor and the Moddell Sector]]" that was also published in ''Star Wars Gamer'' 9 contain several problematic statements on Sarafur's nature as a [[Wikipedia:Tidal locking|tidally locked]] world that [[Wikipedia:Rotation period (astronomy)|rotates]] synchronously with its [[Wikipedia:Orbit|orbit]] around its sun.<ref name="Endor" /> In the latter work, the planet is described as a "world that no longer rotates, spinning around the Tail's yellow sun with one face always locked toward it."<ref name="Endor" /> The statement is self-contradictory—if indeed Sarafur had not been revolving ''around its own [[Wikipedia:Rotation around a fixed axis|axis of rotation]]'', it would have been impossible for the planet to have kept the same side of its surface always oriented toward its sun as it was orbiting it—that is, revolving ''around the star''.<ref name="RotateAndOrbit">{{WebCite|url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Tidal_locking_of_the_Moon_with_the_Earth.gif|text=File:Tidal locking of the Moon with the Earth.gif|author=[[Wikipedia:User:Stigmatella aurantiaca|User:Stigmatella aurantiaca]]|date=2013-05-04|work=[[Wikipedia:Wikipedia|Wikipedia]]|archivedate=20201112004233}} — A visualization of the difference between objects respectively ''rotating'' and ''not rotating'' around their own axes while simultaneously orbiting another object</ref> Furthermore, "Endor and the Moddell Sector" lists Sarafur's local year as being 240 "[[Local day/Legends|local days]]" long<ref name="Endor" /> despite the concept of a local day being undefined in ''[[Star Wars]]'' for tidally locked planets.<ref name="SWPlanets">''[[The Star Wars Planets Collection]]''</ref>

Finally, both articles use incorrect terminology when referring to the region surrounding the border between Sarafur's illuminated and dark sides,<ref name="Endor" /><ref name="Tessent" /> that is, its [[Wikipedia:Terminator (solar)|terminator]].<ref name="Terminator">The term "terminator," used to refer to the border between a celestial body's illuminated and dark sides, appears in several published ''[[Star Wars]]'' works such as ''[[Dark Force Rising]]'' and [[Knight Errant (novel)|''Knight Errant'']].</ref> "Endor and the Moddell Sector" refers to the area as Sarafur's "[[Wikipedia:Equator|equatorial]] region,"<ref name="Endor" /> while "Race for the Tessent," in several instances, calls it the "[[Wikipedia:Event horizon|event horizon]]."<ref name="Tessent" /> Similarly to the real world, in ''Star Wars'', the latter term instead refers to a zone surrounding a [[Black hole/Legends|black hole]].<ref name="TUR" /><ref name="BlackHole">{{WebCite|url=https://www.space.com/black-holes-event-horizon-explained.html|text=What is a black hole event horizon (and what happens there)?|author=Lea, Robert|date=2023-03-03|work=[[Wikipedia:Space.com|Space.com]]|archivedate=20231014181748}}</ref> The former term, in turn, describes a line that is equidistant from a celestial body's northern and southern pole and is perpendicular to its meridians—that is, lines connecting its poles—with one of which the celestial body's terminator must, by its definition, coincide at any given moment in time.<ref name="TUR" /><ref name="Equator">{{WebCite|url=https://www.britannica.com/place/Equator|text=Equator|author=The Editors of Encyclopædia Britannica|date=2023-09-20|work=[[Wikipedia:Encyclopædia Britannica|''Encyclopædia Britannica'']]|archivedate=20231020072656}}</ref><ref name="Meridian">{{WebCite|url=https://www.britannica.com/science/latitude|text=latitude and longitude|author=The Editors of Encyclopædia Britannica|date=2023-09-29|work=[[Wikipedia:Encyclopædia Britannica|''Encyclopædia Britannica'']]|archivedate=20231020231418}}</ref>
